name: Hervidero Waterfall Trail
name_original: Cascadas del Hervidero – running circular desde San Agustín de Guadalix
description: This moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) circular jogging route from San Agustín de Guadalix features the Hervidero Waterfall and river scenery.
region_intro: Jogging routes around San Agustín Del Guadalix traverse a region rich in natural beauty, characterized by its extensive protected natural spaces. The landscape features the Guadalix River, which carves through rocky canyons and forms waterfalls like the Hervidero and El Coso. Runners can explore diverse terrain, including holm oak and cork oak forests, Mediterranean scrub, and serene areas around the Laguna de los Patos. The area also incorporates historic 19th-century aqueducts, offering a blend of natural and historical interest. Best jogging routes around San Agustín Del Guadalix The most popular jogging route is Hervidero Waterfall Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This route leads to the double Hervidero Waterfall, set within a rocky canyon along the Guadalix River. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from San Agustín del Guadalix, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.2 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain and scenic views starting directly from the town. Local runners also love the Plaza de la Constitución – El Coso Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through the river canyon to El Coso Waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Jogging around San Agustín Del Guadalix is defined by river canyons, waterfalls, holm oak forests, and historic aqueducts. The comprehensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2,400 runners have used komoot to explore San Agustín Del Guadalix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: San Agustín Del Guadalix offers a comprehensive network of over 15 running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes, all set within the region's beautiful natural spaces.

Q: Are there running routes that feature waterfalls in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Yes, the area is known for its beautiful waterfalls. You can explore the double &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Guadalix River via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957880552/hervidero-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3974672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Constitución – El Coso Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the picturesque El Coso Waterfall.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while running in this region?
A: Runners in San Agustín Del Guadalix will experience a diverse natural environment. The routes wind through holm oak and cork oak forests, Mediterranean scrub, and dramatic river canyons carved by the Guadalix River. You'll also find serene spots like the Laguna de los Patos and expansive views around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/484448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly running paths in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Yes, the extensive trail network includes options suitable for all levels. While many routes are moderate, there are easy paths perfect for a relaxed jog or a family outing. The 7 km round-trip to the Hervidero Waterfall, for instance, is flat and easily navigable, making it a great scenic option for all.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running paths in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain and scenic views directly from the town.

Q: Are there historical sights or landmarks along the running trails?
A: Yes, the region offers a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. You can encounter 19th-century hydraulic structures of the Canal de Isabel II, including the significant aqueducts of La Retuerta, Valcaliente, and La Sima. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33946735" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Retuerta Aqueduct loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to explore these historical elements.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The running routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Is San Agustín Del Guadalix suitable for running with dogs?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail may vary, the abundant natural spaces and extensive trail network generally make San Agustín Del Guadalix a welcoming area for outdoor activities with pets. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and follow local guidelines to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The region offers beautiful running conditions throughout the year. The autumnal colors around the Hervidero Waterfall are particularly stunning, and the serene reflections at Laguna de los Patos are best enjoyed during cooler months. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures ideal for running, while summer mornings or evenings can also be pleasant.

Q: Are there any routes with good viewpoints or scenic overlooks?
A: Yes, many trails offer excellent views, especially those that traverse the higher ground or river canyons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views of the Sierra de Guadarrama, and the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir (del Vellón)&lt;/a&gt; offers tranquil vistas, perfect for a scenic run.

Q: How long are the running routes typically in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The running routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix vary in length to suit different preferences. Many popular trails range from approximately 7 km (4.4 miles) to over 12 km (7.5 miles). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6937729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Constitución – Charco Dehesa de Moncalvillo loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 12.5 km, offering a longer challenge.
tips:
A beautiful site! The small lake is perfect for disconnecting and stopping in the shade for a snack. Ideal for families.

Be careful when going down the narrow stairs that have no railings. The ascent is a bit poorly indicated, you have to look a little.

faqs.question: Where does the Cascadas del Hervidero running route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route commonly begins at the Area Recreativa de la Laguna de los Patos in San Agustín de Guadalix. This recreational area typically provides parking facilities for visitors.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While parts of the trail are described as 'simple' and feature 'flat terrain,' making them suitable for beginners, other sections include 'positive slopes,' 'very steep paths,' and 'stairs carved into the rock' near the waterfalls. These steeper parts might be challenging for continuous jogging, so beginners should be prepared for varied conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll find wide esplanades and sections through riverine forests, particularly along the Guadalix River. However, be prepared for some 'positive slopes,' 'very steep paths,' and even 'stairs carved into the rock' as you approach the waterfalls. It's not uniformly flat, so some sections may require a slower pace.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route follows the Guadalix River and features the picturesque Hervidero Waterfall, along with another waterfall, the Becerra waterfall. You'll also encounter charming wooden bridges, an old mill, a small dam, and an old metal bridge covered in vegetation. Structures related to the Canal de Isabel II, such as the Azud del Mesto, are also visible.

faqs.question: Is the Cascadas del Hervidero running route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, due to the varied terrain, including steep sections and stairs, and to protect local wildlife, it's advisable to keep your dog on a leash throughout the route.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Cascadas del Hervidero running route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: Spring is particularly beautiful, with meadows adorned with flowers and the river often full after rains. The trail offers scenic views year-round, but spring and autumn generally provide more comfortable temperatures for jogging. After heavy rains, some sections might be muddy or the river flow more intense.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino del Carril de las Mentiras, the Canal de El Mesto, the Camino Canal del Vellón, and the Camino Canal Bajo CYII.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the starting point at Area Recreativa de la Laguna de los Patos may offer some basic facilities, there are no specific restrooms or reliable water sources directly on the trail itself. It's recommended to carry enough water for your run.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, comfortable running shoes with good grip are essential. Bring sufficient water, especially on warmer days, and consider snacks. A phone for navigation and emergencies, and appropriate clothing for the weather, are also recommended. If you plan to explore the steeper sections, trekking poles might be helpful.

faqs.question: Is there an 'insider tip' for enjoying this route more?
faqs.answer: For a more continuous jogging experience, focus on the flatter sections like parts of the 'Camino del Brincadero.' If you want to fully appreciate the waterfalls and their surroundings, be prepared to slow down or walk through the 'stairs carved into the rock' sections, as they are not ideal for uninterrupted running.
